schema_version: v0 — migrates when Q6 (intention envelope) lands
owner: akiendelllinux_cc_0 (CC) — CC gates writes; Akien-ratified (signature gate, 2026-07-21: 'looks good to me'). Name ratified this session: `harbor_master`, from the 'artifact-owner' working label.
intention: The device (`harbor_master`) that owns the HARBOR through which workflows voyage. It grants CLEARANCE for a workflow's state-transition (the authority rung — owner-gated, delegable through its gate) and keeps the AGGREGATE REGISTER of the fleet (the truth rung — what is in port, where each stands, every movement logged). It does NOT own the boats (each workflow is owned by its intention), does NOT execute (the crew/igor sails, under delegated clearance), and does NOT own the rules (transition legality is base-class physics). 'Where you go to query open tickets' — the fleet-scale query surface; individual boats own themselves, the harbor cooperatively manages them in aggregate.
why: A workflow-transition factors into THREE rungs: RULES (is BUILDME->PROVEME legal for this class? — base-class physics, inherited, cannot be delegated away), AUTHORITY (who may invoke it? — owner-gated, delegable per-operation, non-ambient, Law 6), and TRUTH (record it — local in the boat's own `history`, central in the harbor register). The harbor_master owns the authority gate + the central register; the base-class owns the rules; the intention owns the boat + its local history. This gives the emit-chokepoint's record-and-clearance half a HOME without becoming the ground_loop-executor goof: decision local, record central, execution never here. The aggregate register is compiled inference at FLEET scale (Law 1) — 'what is open' answered from structure, not re-derived by walking every boat — the same move as the per-ticket projector (charter-state-history-split), one scale out.
traces_to: Law 6 (exactly one owner; delegated access and ownership transfer only through the owner's gate, never ambiently — the clearance rung)
Law 7 (the harbor register is a record of truth: append-only, an index over the boats' own truths, never a rival that drifts)
Law 4 (the rules are base-class physics, not the harbor's to grant — 'Gates, not supervisors')
Law 1 (the aggregate is compiled, not re-derived — fleet-scale compiled inference)
Law 8 (the method a workflow-string resolves to enters only proven — the method-registry returns here with the clearance gate)
state: code-seam@v1: THINKME -> [TICKETME] -> BUILDME -> PROVEME -> LEARNME -> PROVED   (cursor at TICKETME: design wrapped + ratified + decomposing. NOT buildable — waits on (1) charter-state-history-split, whose local histories it aggregates, and (2) the base-class emit-chokepoint / transition-validation physics per state-machine-physics.json. A parent-infrastructure dependency, like state-machine-physics itself.)
gates: proof gate (tester) — a proof a hollow build could not pass: QUERY the fleet from it (list open workflows, in-port, follow one journey across devices); show a transition requires CLEARANCE (the harbor refuses an unauthorized or unrecorded move — legality itself is refused by the base-class rules, the harbor refuses the un-cleared move); show clearance is DELEGABLE (an igor advances a workflow under grant through the gate, never ambiently — Law 6); show the register is an INDEX over the boats' truths, not a rival record (Law 7). VALIDATION must be PERSISTED via db_domain (store.persist_validation) and grep back. Durability gate: proven, persisted, committed.
falsifier: A works when you can QUERY the fleet from it (open workflows, in-port, a cross-device journey) and it stays honest as an index over the boats' own truths — never a second record that can drift (Law 7). Wrong-intent signals: (1) if it starts EXECUTING or SCHEDULING work, it is the ground_loop-executor goof — the harbor master never sails; (2) if devices advance workflows AROUND it (ambient transitions, no clearance), the authority gate has failed (Law 6); (3) if it comes to OWN the boats (mutating a workflow's own content, not just clearing + logging its movement), it has stopped being the harbor and become the FLEET OWNER — the slip Akien caught in design; (4) if the register holds truths the boats do not (a divergent copy), Law 7 breach.
horizon: first real fleet query — when at least two live workflows are visible in the harbor register with their standings, one journey can be followed across devices (the web_server journey pane, deferred, cashes out here), and a delegated clearance (an igor advancing a workflow) is refused when unauthorized and logged when granted. Re-check then: is the register git-JSON or a db_domain store, and is the local-history / harbor-register split holding as TWO VANTAGES (not duplication)?
children: (waits on charter-state-history-split) a — the HARBOR REGISTER / aggregate: the fleet-scale index over the boats' local histories (compiled, queryable: open workflows, in-port, journeys). Co-resolves the db-vs-git placement edge with the split ticket.

(waits on the base-class emit-chokepoint) b — the CLEARANCE gate: grants/refuses a workflow's transition through the owner's gate (authority rung), delegable to an igor per-operation, non-ambient (Law 6); records the movement (truth rung). The method-registry (Law 8; retired with the driver-executor, cairn 584aa74) RETURNS here — re-instated, not re-derived (Law 1).

(deferred, meets web-server child d) c — the JOURNEY view: follow one journey_id across all devices; this IS the source the web_server journey pane renders (the map's Events-vs-Journeys split: per-device Events = the LOGGING pane; cross-device Journeys = this).
waits_on: charter-state-history-split (the local histories it aggregates) AND the base-class emit-chokepoint / transition-validation (state-machine-physics.json — the rules rung it clears against). Design-complete now; buildable when both land.
provenance: cast 2026-07-21 via /sorted. Born last session as 'artifact-owner' (/intent, held); this session the design converged through a metaphor Akien landed — the HARBOR MASTER: gates movement (clearance), keeps the register, never sails, does not own the boats. The emit-chokepoint 'collision' with state-machine-physics.json resolved into a TRICHOTOMY (rules = base-class / authority = harbor_master, delegable / truth = harbor register + the boat's local history), so state-machine-physics is CROSS-REFERENCED, not overwritten. Ownership resolved: the intention owns its workflow (a boat owns itself); the harbor cooperatively manages the fleet in aggregate ('where we go to query open tickets'). Ratified by Akien: 'looks good to me.'
